title: lithophragma parviflorum var. trifoliatum
intro: Lithophragma parviflorum var. trifoliatum, also known as the small-flowered woodland bells, is a perennial plant in the saxifrage family that grows in the wild in the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lithophragma parviflorum var. trifoliatum include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its traditional use to treat fever, coughs, and other respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although it is essential to consult with a qualified healthcare professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes.
